title: [slowbro] Battle Guide
summary: A [water]/[psychic] physical wall whose [regenerator] heals 33% HP on every switch, looping [future-sight] and [teleport] to control team tempo.
strengths: 110 Defense with HP investment walls physical attackers
weaknesses: Bug, Ghost, Dark, Electric, and Grass weaknesses are all live
Speed 30 loses every race, making taunt a hard counter
Setup sweepers freely boost against it

recommendedMoves.reason: Reliable recovery for the long-game plan
Pivot move that resets matchups while healing

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[slowbro] is a [water]/[psychic] physical wall whose identity revolves around the hidden ability [regenerator] — it restores 33% HP every time it switches out. Combined with 95/110/80 bulk and [slack-off] recovery, [slowbro] is functionally unkillable by physical attackers. Its real win condition comes from the [future-sight] + [teleport] combo, which creates guaranteed team tempo that wins the long game.

## Strengths
[regenerator] makes [slowbro] effectively immortal against physical attackers. Every switch restores 33% HP, which means chip damage never accumulates across a match. 110 Defense with maxed HP walls every physical neutral attack, and [slack-off] provides in-battle recovery on top. [scald] chip damage from the 30% burn chance further weakens opposing physical attackers over time.

## Weaknesses & Answers
Bug, Ghost, Dark, Electric, and Grass are all 2x weaknesses, and common coverage like knock-off bypasses the wall plan by removing [leftovers]. Speed 30 means taunt shuts down every utility slot instantly — a taunt lead is the hardest matchup. Setup sweepers freely boost against [slowbro], so the team must have haze or phazing support to answer them.

## Recommended Moveset
[scald] is the locked STAB because the 30% burn chance passively weakens incoming physical attackers. [future-sight] is the key utility — it predicts future switches and creates guaranteed damage three turns later. [slack-off] is mandatory recovery for long-game stability, and [teleport] is the [regenerator] activator that also brings in a partner on tempo.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[leftovers] is the default because it stacks with [regenerator] for maximum healing. [sitrus-berry] is a mid-game heal alternative for frontal plans. [rocky-helmet] punishes contact attackers for meaningful chip across a match. Ability must be [regenerator] — the other two are not viable. Tera [water] maxes [scald] damage defensively, Tera [fairy] flips Dark and Bug weaknesses into resistances.

## Teammates & Synergy
[slowbro] anchors [future-sight] + [teleport] cores with dedicated finishers (Fighting or [psychic] attackers) that convert the guaranteed damage into KOs. A [fairy] or Steel partner handles Dark and Bug weaknesses, and a Ground partner covers Electric. haze or taunt support answers setup sweepers that would otherwise boost for free.

## Play Patterns
Switch in on a physical attacker, [future-sight] on the free turn, then [teleport] out to bring in a finisher as the [future-sight] lands. Use [scald] on any turn you hold position, heal with [slack-off] at 60% HP, and save Tera for a telegraphed Dark or Bug move.
